error.signature: AI misunderstands Brazilian jeitinho, physical warmth, and carnival-influenced expressiveness

verdict.summary: Brazilian culture values warmth, flexibility (jeitinho), and personal relationships above rigid rules. Physical closeness, cheek kisses, and creative problem-solving are core cultural traits.

dead_ends.action: Maintain strict physical distance or refuse cheek kisses (beijinho) with Brazilians
dead_ends.why_fails: Brazilians greet with cheek kisses (1 in São Paulo, 2 in Rio, 3 in some regions). Pulling away or offering only a handshake to someone of familiar acquaintance signals coldness and rejection.

dead_ends.action: Apply rigid, rule-bound approaches and expect strict deadline adherence
dead_ends.why_fails: Jeitinho brasileiro (the Brazilian way) values creative, flexible solutions over rigid processes. 'Dar um jeito' means finding a way around obstacles. Strict rigidity is seen as lacking creativity.
dead_ends.condition: Business process design for Brazilian teams

dead_ends.action: Make the 'OK' hand gesture (thumb and index finger circle) in Brazil
dead_ends.why_fails: In Brazil, the OK sign is equivalent to the middle finger — one of the most offensive gestures possible. This catches many visitors off guard. Use thumbs up (positive in Brazil) instead.

workarounds.action: Embrace physical warmth: cheek kisses for greetings, hugs (abraço) for friends, close-talking distance. Touch on arm/shoulder during conversation is normal and shows engagement, not invasion.

Build personal relationships before business. Brazilians do business with people they like and trust. Small talk about futebol (soccer), family, and travel is essential before any business discussion.
Understand 'hora brasileira' (Brazilian time): social events start 30-60 minutes late. Business meetings aim for punctuality but flexibility is expected. Never show frustration about timing.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Brazil has unique customs (jeitinho, beijinho count by region, OK gesture taboo) distinct from general Latin American norms
